name: Community First Guam
description_short: Community First Guam is a federally chartered credit union serving Guam since 1962, offering savings, checking, loans, and mortgage services to members.
description_long: Community First Guam Federal Credit Union has operated as a member-owned financial cooperative in Guam for over 60 years. Founded in 1962, the organization positions itself as a community-focused financial institution dedicated to serving the underserved residents and entities of Guam. The credit union is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and maintains a routing number (321480406) for standard banking operations. The institution offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial financial products. Core offerings include savings and checking accounts, personal loans available through online application, auto loans, mortgage loans (marketed as their specialty with award recognition), business services, and instant-issue Visa debit cards available for same-day pickup at their Hagåtña headquarters. Members can access services through multiple locations including their main headquarters, other member service centers, and a newer branch at Micronesia Mall. Digital banking is supported through online banking and a mobile app available on both iOS and Android platforms. Community First distinguishes itself through explicit community commitment and social mission focus. The organization's stated mission emphasizes promoting community development and improving social and economic conditions for underserved populations. The credit union has demonstrated this through partnerships like the Federal Home Loan Bank of Des Moines Member Impact Fund, which awarded $52,500 to three Guam non-profits. The mortgage team earned recognition as 2025 Pika's Best of Guam Winner in the Best Mortgage Lender category and a 2020 Freddie Mac Home Possible RISE Award, positioning mortgages as their institutional strength. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Community First operates under a cooperative structure where members are owners rather than customers. The organization is governed by an elected Board of Directors and includes a Supervisory Committee for member protection. However, membership eligibility appears limited to Guam residents or those with connections to the Guam community, making this a regional institution rather than a nationwide option. The credit union has introduced a paper statement fee effective January 1, 2026, for non-enrolled members, though digital enrollment in online banking and e-statements can avoid this charge.
pros: Over 60 years of community presence and institutional stability in Guam
Award-winning mortgage team with 2025 Pika's Best of Guam recognition and Freddie Mac RISE Award
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institutional profit with member interests
Multiple convenient branch locations including newer Micronesia Mall branch
Instant-issue Visa debit cards available for same-day pickup at headquarters
Active community investment through partnerships (e.g., $52,500 Member Impact Fund distribution)
Digital-first options with mobile banking app and online loan applications
cons: Geographically limited to Guam; not a nationwide option for other states
Membership eligibility restricted to Guam residents or community-connected individuals
Paper statement fees effective January 1, 2026 incentivize digital-only banking
Limited transparency on specific loan terms, rates, or product details from public website
No evidence of specialized products for credit building or financial inclusion beyond general lending
best_for: Guam residents seeking comprehensive retail banking with local community focus
Homebuyers in Guam interested in mortgage services from award-winning lenders
Business owners on Guam needing credit union business services and local relationships
Members prioritizing cooperative ownership structure and community development impact
diagnosis: Community First Guam is best for Guam residents seeking full-service banking with a community-focused, member-owned institution—particularly those interested in mortgages from an award-winning team. The primary caveat is geographic limitation: membership is restricted to Guam, making this unsuitable for mainland U.S. consumers seeking a credit union partner.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
